assertions.description: crypto.subtle.encapsulateKey is a function — KEM encapsulation producing an EncapsulatedKey (sharedKey CryptoKey + ciphertext).
crypto.subtle.encapsulateBits is a function — KEM encapsulation producing raw shared-secret bits + ciphertext.
crypto.subtle.decapsulateKey is a function — reverses encapsulation to a shared-secret CryptoKey.
crypto.subtle.decapsulateBits is a function — reverses encapsulation to raw bits.
crypto.subtle.getPublicKey is a function — derives the public CryptoKey from an asymmetric private key with chosen usages.
The static SubtleCrypto.supports exists on the interface object — the spec's feature-detection method, shipped in Chromium under the same RuntimeEnabled=WebCryptoPQC gate (subtle_crypto.idl).

The partial SubtleCrypto interface is [SecureContext]: on a non-secure context the new methods must be absent (on a secure context either state is spec-legal, since the runtime feature also gates them).

When the static interface method is present, SubtleCrypto.supports('generateKey', 'ML-KEM-768') — a name registered in spec section 6.2 — answers with a real boolean (never undefined/throw), so callers can rely on it before attempting key generation.
assertions.test: (()=>{ if (typeof SubtleCrypto.supports !== 'function') return false; try { const r = SubtleCrypto.supports('generateKey', 'ML-KEM-768'); return typeof r === 'boolean'; } catch (e) { return false; } })()
